ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general latte beverage class; no specific hazelnut-flavoured latte subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no hazelnut latte-specific code exists. Closest defensible category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for hazelnut latte beverage.

allergens: tree nuts (hazelnut — depending on syrup formulation)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard medium-sized hazelnut latte served in a café cup of approximately 240 ml, equivalent to roughly 240 g.

nutrient_source.title: Hazelnut Latte — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Hazelnut Latte (cr_id G00010969). All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference for café latte with flavoured syrup (USDA SR Legacy item 14209 adapted) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; café latte / milk-based coffee beverage analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for hazelnut latte specifically. GI of 41 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for café latte / milk-based sweetened coffee beverages (GI range 35–47, midpoint ~41). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (41) × available carbohydrate per serving (4.35 g/100g × 240 g serving = 10.44 g) ÷ 100 = 4.3 g. Classified as low GI (<55).

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar from hazelnut syrup; sodium 35 mg/100 g; potassium 152 mg/100 g (below 200 mg offset threshold); sweetened flavoured beverage classification
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or limit sweetened flavoured lattes due to added sugar content; request sugar-free hazelnut syrup or switch to an unsweetened plain latte to reduce cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from hazelnut syrup (sugar 4.06 g/100 g); sugar-sweetened beverage classification triggers dietary caution for blood glucose management
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid hazelnut latte made with standard hazelnut syrup due to added sugar content; a sugar-free syrup variant or plain unsweetened latte is a clinically preferable alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar from hazelnut syrup; saturated fat approximately 0.9 g/100 g from whole milk; cholesterol 7 mg/100 g; low dietary fibre (0 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit sweetened flavoured coffee beverages; the combination of added sugar, saturated fat from whole milk, and negligible fibre is unfavourable for lipid and glycaemic management — substituting sugar-free syrup and low-fat milk reduces cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hazelnut Latte is a popular espresso-based coffee beverage made by combining a shot or double shot of espresso with steamed whole milk and hazelnut-flavoured syrup. The result is a warm, creamy, mildly sweet drink with a distinctive roasted coffee flavour complemented by the sweet, nutty aroma of hazelnut. The texture is smooth and velvety due to the steamed milk, and the beverage is typically served in a medium-sized café cup of approximately 240 ml. In Singapore and internationally, hazelnut latte is a common café menu item consumed at breakfast or as a mid-morning or afternoon snack. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 43 kcal, 2.81 g protein, 1.61 g fat, and 4.35 g carbohydrate, with 4.06 g sugar derived primarily from the hazelnut syrup. It contains 105 mg calcium and 152 mg potassium per 100 g, reflecting its milk content. Dietary fibre is negligible at 0 g/100 g. The beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened flavoured coffee drink and is not recommended for individuals with type 2 diabetes or hypertension without modification to a sugar-free syrup variant. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 41 (low GI), but the added sugar content remains a clinical concern for metabolic conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a hazelnut latte?
Is hazelnut latte suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of hazelnut latte?
Is hazelnut latte high in calories?
Can I drink hazelnut latte if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of hazelnut latte?
Is hazelnut latte suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010969, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat and fatty acid breakdown estimated from USDA SR Legacy closest analogue and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous milk-based coffee beverage entry with full documentation.
notes: Saturated fat (0.9 g/100g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.4 g/100g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.07 g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for café latte (item 14209) as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown for this item; values are approximate and should be updated when HPB publishes detailed fatty acid data. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g/100g (confirmed negligible for standard milk-based coffee beverages per USDA SR Legacy). Dietary fibre is 0.0 g/100g as confirmed by HPB source — this is plausible for a liquid milk-coffee-syrup beverage with no solid fibre-containing ingredients. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed vitamin, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific hazelnut latte formulation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry and are not available from a directly applicable approved source as of 2026-04-16. Hazelnut latte is classified as a sugar-sweetened flavoured beverage (SSB) due to the addition of hazelnut syrup containing added sugar; this classification triggers dietary caution gates for type 2 diabetes and hypertension regardless of the absolute sugar figure per 100 g.
